most_recent_game.recap_text:
CHICAGO, Ill. -
Despite seven RBIs on the day from
Jacob McKenzie
, Southern could not close out UIC and suffered two walk-off losses Saturday in Chicago.
Game One - UIC 10, Southern Illinois 9
After Friday's pitcher's duel, both teams flipped the script in game one, trading blows early.
Jackson Dibble
countered Alexander Wright's solo home run with his first of the season in the third inning. The Flames immediately went back in front, but
Jacob McKenzie
announced his arrival with a two-run home run in the fourth.
The Salukis seemingly grew tired of trading blows, as another UIC run in the fourth tied the game at three. In the fifth,
Julio Guerrero
retook the lead with a solo home run, his second of the season.
Jaden Flores
beat out an infield single with the bases loaded, and with two runners still in scoring position, McKenzie added two more with a single.
Dawson Hargrove
helped the staff settle in the middle innings, giving up just one run on three innings pitched and helping his team hold a 7-4 lead heading into the final three frames.
In the blink of an eye, UIC brought the game within one as Jake Busson and Thomas Curry hit back-to-back home runs to open the bottom of the seventh.
In the midst of the critical snag from the SIU bullpen, three straight singles and a walk would tie the game, with a hit-by-pitch plating the go-ahead run. UIC ended the inning with five runs, facing three Saluki relievers.
A scoreless eighth from
Charlie Miller
kept the Dawgs within one at 8-7, setting up
Jacob McKenzie
for his third clutch hit of the game. His two-out RBI double scored
Bryce Habuda
to tie the game in the ninth.
With pinch-runner
Kristian Sprawling
on third,
Kye Watson
slapped a line drive that popped out of the third baseman's glove, giving him just enough time to beat the throw and for Sprawling to score the go-ahead run.
On the second pitch of the bottom of the ninth, Ashton Kampa knotted the game up at nine with a solo home run. Another hit-by-pitch allowed UIC to play it safe with a sacrifice bunt to move the runner to second. With one away, Jake Busson delivered for the Flames with a walk-off single to left, ending the nearly four-hour-long game at 10-9.
Charlie Miller
(L, 0-1) took the loss for SIU, while Mark Eddie (W, 1-1) was credited with the win.
Game Two - UIC 6, Southern Illinois 5
Having used four relief arms in game one of the doubleheader, Head Coach
Lance Rhodes
needed a long and efficient performance from starter
Troy Shepard
.
Shepard grinded out 7.1 innings in the contest, and familiar faces made big plays on offense to support the left-hander.
After UIC struck first in the second,
Tim Simay's
two-run single put Southern ahead 2-1 in the third.
The Flames tacked on their second run in the fourth, but Shepard kept them largely at bay in the middle innings. McKenzie rewarded Shepard's effort with his sixth and seventh RBIs of the day on a two-run double in the sixth to put SIU up 4-2.
While Guerrero did not torture Flames with his third homer of the weekend, he reverted back to wreaking havoc on the basepaths, as an overthrow on his attempt to steal third gave Shepard another run to work with in the seventh.
Through seven innings, Shepard racked up eight strikeouts with just one walk, allowing one earned run on three hits. In the bottom of the eighth, the Coeur d' Alene, Idaho native took the mound having thrown 104 pitches. After surrendering a leadoff walk and an infield single, he collected his ninth strikeout. On his 120th pitch, Vidal Colon tied the game with a three-run home run.
SIU threatened, but could not cash in a run in the top of the ninth.
The game was primed for extra innings, but Busson played hero for the second game, blasting a solo home run to deliver the second brutal loss of the day for Southern in a 6-5 loss to UIC.
After being swept in the doubleheader, SIU falls to 1-2 in MVC play and 9-13 overall.
UP NEXT
Southern Illinois looks for revenge when it heads to UT Martin on Tuesday night. First pitch is scheduled for 5 p.m.
FOLLOW THE SALUKIS
Keep up with all the latest news and information on the Salukis by following the team on Twitter at
@SIU_Baseball
, on Facebook at
Facebook.com/SalukiBaseball
, and on Instagram at
@SIU_Baseball
. Fans can also download the Salukis Mobile App on the
App Store
or
Google Play Store
.
